title: rubus ulmifolius var. brachiatus
intro: Rubus ulmifolius var. brachiatus, also known as the bristly blackberry, is a variety of the bramble plant found in Europe and North Africa. This plant is a type of shrub that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Rubus ulmifolius var. brachiatus include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for its medicinal use, and can also be used as a tincture or added to herbal remedies.
